metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3, replace 'confirm this id resolves in the store' with a concrete proving command (e.g. `ideas get 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` or equivalent) AND state the fallback action if resolution fails (e.g. 'if not found, create the idea stub before merging ADR-0042').
findings.finding: 'Confirm this id resolves in the store' names no system ('the store'), provides no lookup command, and specifies no fallback if the id is absent — an executor cannot complete AC3 without guessing which system to query.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'confirm this id resolves in the store'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Verify with `<store-lookup-command> 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` returns a non-error result; if the id is absent, create the stub in the ideas store and update the ADR anchor before this ticket is complete.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC3, either enumerate the outcome enum values inline (e.g. `LANDED | CONFLICT | CI_FAILURE | TIMEOUT`) or add an explicit pointer to the S4 spec section that defines them, so the executor can transcribe the canonical set.
findings.finding: The 'outcome enum' the ADR must record is not defined in the plan, nor is a pointer to where its canonical definition lives (presumably S4); an executor writing ADR-0042 must guess the enum values or hunt across tickets.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'the agent contract + outcome enum'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC2, e.g.: `git grep -rn -i 'two green votes\|watch votes' -- '*.md' | grep -c .` must output `0` after all edits, to produce falsifiable evidence of exhaustiveness.
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for the exhaustiveness claim in AC2 ('grep the whole repo… update ALL occurrences'): without a final grep that asserts zero remaining matches, an executor cannot produce evidence that the deprecated pattern has been fully eradicated.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verify with `git grep -rni "two green votes\|watch votes" -- "*.md"` returning no output before marking this AC complete.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback clause to the ADR-0042 AC: if idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` does not resolve in the store, record the deferred provider-seam as `TBD-provider-seam` and open a tracking issue — do not leave the anchor blank or pointing at a non-existent entry.
findings.finding: The AC requires anchoring ADR-0042 to idea ID `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` and says 'confirm this id resolves in the store,' but specifies no fallback action if the id does not resolve — leaving the implementer to either ship a broken reference or invent an ad-hoc resolution without guidance.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — ADR-0042 bullet: 'confirm this id resolves in the store and anchor ADR-0042 to it'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC bullet to read: '…anchor ADR-0042 to it; if the id does not resolve, record the reference as `TBD-provider-seam` (open a tracking issue) and note the gap explicitly in the ADR rather than silently omitting the anchor.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC that blocks merge until S1 and S4 are verifiably live (e.g., 'Confirm the `Autosubmit` label (S1) exists in the repo and the `land` command (S4) responds correctly before this PR is merged').
findings.finding: The plan states in prose that 'docs must not merge to main until S1 (Autosubmit label) and S4 (land command) are actually live,' but no Acceptance Criterion encodes this merge-gate as an observable, verifiable condition. An agent executing the plan could produce fully compliant docs and attempt to land them while S1/S4 are still absent — exactly the broken state the plan warns against.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Pre-merge gate: verify `Autosubmit` label is present in GitHub labels (S1 complete) AND `land` command is reachable/documented in the repo (S4 complete); record confirmation in PR description.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the AC2 grep patterns ('two green votes' / 'watch votes') with the actual phrases present in the repo: 'both votes are green', 'gated by green votes', 'both are +1', 'both.*+1', and enumerate the known files that contain old-contract language (docs/your-first-change.md, CONTRIBUTING.md §2e, CLAUDE.md §Git-workflow, examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md) so the sweep is exhaustive.
findings.finding: The plan's AC2 instructs executors to 'Grep the whole repo' for the deprecated 'two green votes' / 'watch votes' pattern, but neither phrase appears verbatim anywhere in the codebase. The actual deprecated landing language uses different phrasing: 'both votes are green' (docs/your-first-change.md lines 98-99, 103; CONTRIBUTING.md §2e), 'gated by green votes' (examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md line 51), 'both are +1' (CLAUDE.md line 539). An executor following the plan's grep literally would find zero matches and declare the sweep complete, missing real occurrences of the old contract that still require updating.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('Grep the whole repo for the deprecated "two green votes" / "watch votes" pattern')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, replace the example grep patterns with: `grep -rn --include='*.md' -E 'both votes are green|gated by green votes|both are .\+1|two.*votes.*green|green votes' .` — and explicitly name the known files (docs/your-first-change.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md) as the confirmed hit-set that must be updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` to the explicit edit-target list in the plan (or extend AC2's grep pattern to cover 'green votes', 'gates are green', and 'land autonomously'), and rewrite its landing-contract prose (lines ~51-55, 84-85, 265-273) to the new 'set Autosubmit / run land / act on single typed outcome' model.
findings.finding: examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md explicitly teaches the old 'green votes → land autonomously' landing contract (lines 51-55: 'Landing is gated by green votes, not by a human... When the gates are green the skill lands autonomously'; lines 84-85: 'land: submit the top of the stack once every change is LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1'; lines 265-273: 'when every change is green... land them together, autonomously') — but the plan never names this file as a consumer requiring update. AC2's repo-wide grep obligation would surface it, but the plan's edit-target enumeration (CLAUDE.md + CONTRIBUTING.md + grep for 'two green votes'/'watch votes') uses search strings that will NOT match this file's phrasing ('green votes', 'when the gates are green', 'both are +1'). An executor following AC2's grep literally will miss this file.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2's grep query to include the phrases 'green votes', 'gates are green', 'land autonomously', and 'both are +1' (in addition to 'two green votes'/'watch votes'), or name examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md explicitly as a required edit target with the specific sections to update.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` resolves in the rebar store (e.g., `rebar show 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`) and record the result in the plan before execution; if the id does not resolve, either create the idea ticket and record the new id, or update AC3 with the correct id.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 instructs the author to 'confirm this id resolves in the store' for idea ID 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984, but the plan itself provides no evidence that the confirmation has been done or is doable. The id has no footprint anywhere in the repo files, making it impossible for a reviewer to verify the anchor is valid before the ADR is written. If the id does not resolve, ADR-0042 cannot anchor to it and the plan's AC3 self-referentially fails.
findings.location: AC3: 'provider-seam deferred to idea 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984 — confirm this id resolves in the store'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md` to the AC2 sweep scope (or widen the grep pattern to catch 'two votes' landing descriptions), and update its landing-procedure prose to the new Autosubmit/land contract.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md (line 234) contains a 'two votes' landing description ('The gate — two votes — the review-bot casts LLM-Review...and CI casts Verified') that teaches the old vote-watching model. This file is not named as a consumer in the plan and uses phrasing that would not be caught by the AC2 grep strings ('two green votes' / 'watch votes').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Broaden AC2's sweep pattern to match the actual deprecated language in the repo: replace the grep terms `"two green votes"` / `"watch votes"` with patterns that cover the real occurrences — e.g. `"both votes are green"`, `"both.*+1"`, `"votes are green"`, or enumerate the specific files to audit (docs/your-first-change.md, docs/review-policy.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) with the actual phrases to update.
findings.finding: The grep pattern specified in AC2 ('two green votes' / 'watch votes') matches zero occurrences in any docs file in the repo today — those exact phrases do not exist. The actual deprecated language is 'both votes are green' (docs/your-first-change.md lines 98, 103) and 'both are +1' (CONTRIBUTING.md §2c). An executor who runs the prescribed grep finds nothing, concludes the sweep is done, and ships S6 while your-first-change.md still teaches the old contract — the problem the plan was written to fix.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Grep the whole repo for the deprecated "two green votes" / "watch votes" pattern (e.g. in docs/your-first-change.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: AC2 rewrite: 'Audit `docs/your-first-change.md`, `docs/review-policy.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and `CLAUDE.md` for the deprecated polling model — including phrases like "both votes are green", "repeat until both are +1", and equivalent — and update ALL occurrences to the new contract (set `Autosubmit` / run `land`; act on the typed outcome; do NOT poll/watch votes).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fifth required element to AC3: ADR-0042 must include an **alternatives-considered section** explicitly dismissing (a) the Gerrit `autosubmit` submit-plugin and (b) the Gerrit built-in Change Queue / Submit Queue (≥3.7), with the same cost/fit reasoning that ADR-0040 applied when it dismissed Zuul.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to record 'the auto-lander, the agent contract + outcome enum, the staged serial→batch plan, and the separation-of-concerns boundary' — but does not require an alternatives-considered section that documents why Gerrit's own native tooling (the `autosubmit` submit-plugin and the built-in Change Queue / Submit Queue available since Gerrit 3.7) was rejected in favour of the bespoke Python auto-lander. Multiple prior-review cache entries on sibling plans (S1/S3) flag exactly this gap. ADR-0042 is the canonical place to record this justification, but the AC as written would allow an ADR that omits it entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: '…and an **Alternatives considered** section that names and dismisses (a) the Gerrit `autosubmit` submit-plugin and (b) the Gerrit built-in Change Queue (Gerrit ≥ 3.7), explaining why neither was adopted (self-hosted version constraints, feature fit, ops cost, or other grounded rationale).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before authoring the `CLAUDE.md`/`CONTRIBUTING.md` contract text, read S4's interface spec and record the exact output surface (`land` subcommand signature, outcome enum values, and how an agent reads them — exit code vs. stdout field) so the docs match the real command.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 instructs agents to 'run `land`' and 'act on the single typed outcome' without specifying S4's actual output interface (exit-code contract, stdout format, or the specific field carrying the typed outcome enum). The `rebar land` subcommand does not yet exist in the codebase, so the exact CLI surface is currently Unverified. If the docs land with a description of the outcome-reading contract that diverges from S4's real interface (e.g., docs say 'inspect stdout JSON' but S4 uses exit codes, or the enum values differ), agents will silently misread outcomes — the same babysitting problem the plan is meant to fix.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria §1 — 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`; act on the single typed outcome'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite note in the plan: 'Docs author must read S4's interface spec (the `land` command's exit-code/output contract and the exact outcome-enum string values) before writing the agent-contract prose in AC1, and must cross-check the written outcome table against S4's AC.' Alternatively, add to AC1: 'outcome enum values and reading mechanism (exit code / stdout field) must match S4's verified interface spec — cite the S4 ticket or ADR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the outcome enum value vocabulary (at minimum: `merged`, `needs_rebase`, `ci_failed`, `review_failed`) and the per-value action each requires to AC1's scope for the CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite — not only to the ADR.
findings.finding: The plan's AC1 instructs the CLAUDE.md/CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite to tell agents to 'act on the single typed outcome' but never defines the outcome enum's value vocabulary anywhere in the plan. An executor writing the agent-facing docs from AC1 alone has no authoritative list of values (e.g. merged | needs_rebase | ci_failed | review_failed) to document — the values appear only in the idea ticket 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984 (status: idea, undesigned) and are deferred to ADR-0042 (which doesn't yet exist). AC1 does not require the CLAUDE.md rewrite to include the enum values, only to reference 'the single typed outcome.'
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('act on the single typed outcome')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to read: '…act on the single typed outcome (enum: `merged` — done; `needs_rebase` — rebase and re-push; `ci_failed` — fix CI; `review_failed` — address findings); do NOT watch votes…' so the per-value action contract is explicit in the agent-facing docs, not only in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either inline the staged serial→batch plan outline in the S6 plan body (or reference the S-series ticket that defines it) so an executor writing ADR-0042 has authoritative source material for that section.
findings.finding: AC3 requires ADR-0042 to document 'the staged serial→batch plan' but the plan body never specifies what that staged plan IS — its phases, the trigger condition for promotion from serial to batch, or the scope of each stage. An executor writing the ADR section has no source material to draw from within this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback clause to the 'confirm this id resolves' sub-step in AC3 — e.g. 'if it does not resolve, surface it as a blocker before writing the ADR rather than silently omitting the anchor.'
findings.finding: AC3 includes an inline verification task — 'confirm this id resolves in the store' for idea ID `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984` — but specifies no fallback if the id does not resolve. The plan provides no contingency (create the ticket? block the PR? use a different anchor?), leaving the executor without a defined protocol for the failure case.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('confirm this id resolves in the store')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3: '…confirm this id resolves in the store (`show_ticket 1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`); if it does not resolve, treat this as a blocker and surface it before merging ADR-0042…'

findings.finding: AC1 instructs the rewritten docs to tell agents to 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`', but neither the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label nor a `land` command exist anywhere in the codebase (infra/gerrit/project.config has no Autosubmit label; no 'land' command appears in any source file). These are deliverables of the parent epic f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af, which is still `open` and unimplemented. The plan declares no dependency on that epic, so docs written under this plan would document non-existent infrastructure and deliver incorrect agent guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope or AC1: 'This plan is a child of epic f1fa; it must not be merged until the `Autosubmit` label and `land` command are live in production. Add a `depends_on` link to the implementing stories.'

findings.finding: The AC instructs that CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md be rewritten to tell agents to 'set `Autosubmit` / run `land`', but neither the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label nor the `rebar land` command exists anywhere in the codebase (absent from infra/gerrit/project.config and from src/). An executor following this AC will write docs that reference non-existent infrastructure, producing a landing section that is immediately broken for any agent who tries to follow it.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
